msgs.text:
# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 1

You will tackle a single open research problem.
End every response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT and a tag:
  [CONTINUE] — more work needed
  [BREAKTHROUGH] — substantive new result, keep going
  [STUCK] — no progress, no obvious next step
  [DONE] — problem resolved or fully exhausted

The attached files are:
- `mega_prompt.md` — the shared protocol / success criterion / framing
- `problem_statement.md` — the verbatim problem
- any paper PDFs (if attached)

Read every attached file before answering. Begin Turn 1.

## Turn 1 — Proposition 3.4 is false as formally stated

The hypotheses in Proposition 3.4 do not determine the **marked** Demushkin group. They miss the full canonical orientation character. 

Let
\[
F=\langle A,S,Y\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2},
\qquad
r_0=A^2S^4[S,Y],
\]
and define the free pro-\(2\) automorphism
\[
\psi(A)=AY^{-4},\qquad
\psi(S)=SY^2,\qquad
\psi(Y)=Y.
\]
Its inverse is given by \(A\mapsto AY^4,\ S\mapsto SY^{-2},\ Y\mapsto Y\). Put
\[
r=\psi^{-1}(r_0)
   =(AY^4)^2(SY^{-2})^4[SY^{-2},Y].
\]

This \(r\) satisfies every stated hypothesis of Proposition 3.4.

First, \(\psi\) identifies the quotients:
\[
F/\langle\!\langle r\rangle\!\rangle
 \;\cong\;
F/\langle\!\langle r_0\rangle\!\rangle.
\]
Hence \(r\) is a rank-three local dyadic Demushkin relator in precisely the same abstract sense as \(r_0\). Labute’s classification gives \(r_0\) as the rank-three \(q=2\) standard relation and identifies its orientation as the intrinsic dualizing character. 

Second, in the free abelianization,
\[
\psi_{\mathrm{ab}}(\bar A)=\bar A-4\bar Y,\qquad
\psi_{\mathrm{ab}}(\bar S)=\bar S+2\bar Y,
\]
so
\[
\psi_{\mathrm{ab}}(2\bar A+4\bar S)
 =2(\bar A-4\bar Y)+4(\bar S+2\bar Y)
 =2\bar A+4\bar S.
\]
Thus
\[
r^{\mathrm{ab}}=2\bar A+4\bar S,
\qquad r\in\ker\rho\cap F_2.
\]

Third, \(r\) has the required degree-two initial form. Indeed,
\[
Y^4\in F_4,\qquad Y^2\in F_2,
\]
and modulo \(F_3\),
\[
(AY^4)^2\equiv A^2,\qquad
(SY^{-2})^4\equiv1,\qquad
[SY^{-2},Y]\equiv[S,Y].
\]
Therefore
\[
\operatorname{in}_2(r)=A^2+[S,Y].
\]

### The obstruction

Write
\[
D_0=F/\langle\!\langle r_0\rangle\!\rangle,\qquad
D_r=F/\langle\!\langle r\rangle\!\rangle.
\]
For \(D_0\), the canonical Demushkin orientation has
\[
\chi_0(A)=-1,\qquad
\chi_0(S)=1,\qquad
\chi_0(Y)=\eta:=(-3)^{-1}.
\]
These are exactly the values recorded in the manuscript. 

The isomorphism \(\bar\psi:D_r\to D_0\) transports the intrinsic orientation, so
\[
\chi_r(S)
 =\chi_0(\psi(S))
 =\chi_0(SY^2)
 =\eta^2.
\]
But
\[
\eta^2=\frac1{9}\neq1.
\]

Now suppose Proposition 3.4 were true. There would be an automorphism
\[
\varphi\in\operatorname{Aut}(F),\qquad
\rho\circ\varphi=\rho,
\]
sending \(\langle\!\langle r\rangle\!\rangle\) to
\(\langle\!\langle r_0\rangle\!\rangle\). It would induce an isomorphism
\(D_r\to D_0\) acting identically on the marked abelianization \(B\). Since the canonical orientation is intrinsic and factors through \(B\), this would force
\[
\chi_r(S)=\chi_0(S)=1,
\]
contradicting \(\chi_r(S)=\eta^2\neq1\).

Hence:

\[
\boxed{\text{Proposition 3.4 is false as stated.}}
\]

### Required repair

At minimum, Proposition 3.4 must assume that the canonical orientation of the marked quotient is the prescribed character
\[
\chi_r(\bar A)=-1,\qquad
\chi_r(\bar S)=1,\qquad
\chi_r(\bar Y)=(-3)^{-1}.
\]
The actual local relator arising from
\[
\bar a=\operatorname{rec}(-4),\qquad
\bar s=\operatorname{rec}(1/2),\qquad
\bar y=\operatorname{rec}(-3)
\]
does have these values, so this counterexample does **not** refute the candidate presentation. It refutes the universal normalization lemma used to prove it.

There is also a cleaner replacement target. Put
\[
t=\bar A+2\bar S.
\]
Then
\[
B\cong C_2t\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar S\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar Y,
\]
and
\[
\nu_{\rm ur}(t,\bar S,\bar Y)=(0,1,0),\qquad
\chi(t,\bar S,\bar Y)=(-1,1,\eta).
\]
Thus
\[
(\nu_{\rm ur},\chi):B\longrightarrow
\mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times
\]
is injective, as the manuscript also observes.  Consequently it is enough to prove a **\(\nu_{\rm ur}\)-relative Labute normalization**: every group isomorphism automatically preserves the canonical \(\chi\), and preserving \(\nu_{\rm ur}\) would then force identity on all of \(B\).

ESTABLISHED — Proposition 3.4 is false as stated; the explicit relator above is a counterexample, and the missing invariant is the marked canonical orientation character.  
GAP — It remains to prove that adding the correct orientation, or merely requiring preservation of \(\nu_{\rm ur}\), is sufficient for normalization to \(A^2S^4[S,Y]\).  
NEXT — Rework Labute’s successive-correction argument with all corrections constrained to preserve \(\nu_{\rm ur}\); intrinsic preservation of \(\chi\) and injectivity of \((\nu_{\rm ur},\chi)\) would then recover the full marked statement.  
[BREAKTHROUGH]
